name: Kung Pao Beef
recipeIngredient: 1 lb Beef (boneless sirloin or flank steak)
recipeInstructions:
1. Trim fat from beef steak; cut beef into 3/4-inch cubes.
2. Toss beef, 1 tablespoon oil, cornstarch, salt, and white pepper in a glass or plastic bowl. Cover and refrigerate for 30 minutes.
3. Cut chilies into thin slices (remove seeds and membrane if desired). Cut onions diagonally into 1-inch pieces. Cut bell pepper into 3/4 inch squares.
4. Heat a 12-inch skillet or wok until very hot. Add 2 tablespoons oil; rotate skillet to coat the bottom.
5. Add beef; stir-fry 2 minutes or until brown. Remove beef from skillet.
6. Heat skillet until very hot. Add 2 tablespoons oil; rotate skillet to coat the bottom.
7. Add chilies, garlic, ginger root, bean sauce, and bamboo shoots; stir-fry 1 minute.
8. Add beef, bell pepper, and sugar; stir-fry 1 minute.
9. Stir in onions. Sprinkle with peanuts.
